lines.english: When the Divine Guru grants His Grace, one looks upon good and bad as the same.
When the Divine Guru grants His Grace, the wall of the body is not eroded.
When the Divine Guru grants His Grace, the temple turns itself towards the mortal.
When the Divine Guru grants His Grace, one has good destiny written on his forehead. ||5||
When the Divine Guru grants His Grace, one's home is constructed.
When the Divine Guru grants His Grace, one has bathed at the sixty-eight sacred shrines of pilgrimage.
When the Divine Guru grants His Grace, one's bed is lifted up out of the water. ||6||
When the Divine Guru grants His Grace, all poison is transformed into fruit. ||7||
When the Divine Guru grants His Grace, one's body is stamped with the sacred mark of Vishnu.
When the Divine Guru grants His Grace, one has performed the twelve devotional services.
When the Divine Guru grants His Grace, skepticism is shattered.
When the Divine Guru grants His Grace, one crosses over the terrifying world-ocean.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਉ ਗੁਰਦੇਉ ਤ ਜਮ ਤੇ ਛੂਟੈ ॥
lines.translit: jau guradeo ta jam te chhoottai |
lines.english: When the Divine Guru grants His Grace, one escapes from the Messenger of Death.
When the Divine Guru grants His Grace, one understands the rituals of the eighteen Puraanas.
When the Divine Guru grants His Grace, it is as if one has made an offering of the eighten loads of vegetation.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਉ ਗੁਰਦੇਉ ਤ ਜਨਮਿ ਨ ਮਰੈ ॥੮॥
lines.translit: jau guradeo ta janam na marai |8|
lines.english: When the Divine Guru grants His Grace, one is not subject to the cycle of reincarnation. ||8||
When the Divine Guru grants His Grace, one needs no other place of rest.
Naam Dayv has entered the Sanctuary of the Guru. ||9||1||2||11||

lines.gurmukhi: ਭੈਰਉ ਬਾਣੀ ਰਵਿਦਾਸ ਜੀਉ ਕੀ ਘਰੁ ੨ ॥
lines.translit: bhairau baanee ravidaas jeeo kee ghar 2 |
lines.english: Bhairao, The Word Of Ravi Daas Jee, Second House:
One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:
Whoever chants and praises the Naam, the Name of the Lord,
Whatever is seen, shall pass away.
Without seeing something, the yearning for it does not arise.
is the true Yogi, free of desire. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਮੁਨਿ ਮਨ ਕੀ ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਖਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: so mun man kee dubidhaa khaae |
lines.english: He alone is a silent sage, who destroys the duality of his mind.
When someone utters the Name of the Lord with love,

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਾਰਸੁ ਪਰਸੈ ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਨ ਹੋਈ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: paaras parasai dubidhaa na hoee |1| rahaau |
lines.english: it is as if he has touched the philosopher's stone; his sense of duality is eradicated. ||1||Pause||
Keeping the doors of his body closed, he merges in the Lord of the three worlds.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨ ਕਾ ਸੁਭਾਉ ਸਭੁ ਕੋਈ ਕਰੈ ॥
lines.translit: man kaa subhaau sabh koee karai |
lines.english: Everyone acts according to the inclinations of the mind.
Attuned to the Creator Lord, one remains free of fear. ||2||
Plants blossom forth to produce fruit.
For the sake of spiritual wisdom, people act and practice rituals.
When the fruit is produced, the flowers wither away.
When spiritual wisdom wells up, then actions are left behind. ||3||
Those who are Jivan-mukta, liberated while yet alive - are forever in the state of Nirvaanaa.
For the sake of ghee, wise people churn milk.
Naam Dayv:
Says Ravi Daas, O you unfortunate people,

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਿਦੈ ਰਾਮੁ ਕੀ ਨ ਜਪਸਿ ਅਭਾਗ ॥੪॥੧॥
lines.translit: ridai raam kee na japas abhaag |4|1|
lines.english: why not meditate on the Lord with love in your heart? ||4||1||
Come, O Lord of beautiful hair,

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨਿ ਆਕਾਸ ਕੁਲਹ ਸਿਰਿ ਕੀਨੀ ਕਉਸੈ ਸਪਤ ਪਯਾਲਾ ॥
lines.translit: jin aakaas kulah sir keenee kausai sapat payaalaa |
lines.english: Your cap is the realm of the Akaashic ethers; the seven nether worlds are Your sandals.
wearing the robes of a Sufi Saint. ||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਮਰ ਪੋਸ ਕਾ ਮੰਦਰੁ ਤੇਰਾ ਇਹ ਬਿਧਿ ਬਨੇ ਗੁਪਾਲਾ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: chamar pos kaa mandar teraa ih bidh bane gupaalaa |1|
lines.english: The body covered with skin is Your temple; You are so beautiful, O Lord of the World. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਛਪਨ ਕੋਟਿ ਕਾ ਪੇਹਨੁ ਤੇਰਾ ਸੋਲਹ ਸਹਸ ਇਜਾਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: chhapan kott kaa pehan teraa solah sehas ijaaraa |
lines.english: The fifty-six million clouds are Your gowns, the 16,000 milkmaids are your skirts.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਾਰ ਅਠਾਰਹ ਮੁਦਗਰੁ ਤੇਰਾ ਸਹਨਕ ਸਭ ਸੰਸਾਰਾ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: bhaar atthaarah mudagar teraa sahanak sabh sansaaraa |2|
lines.english: The eighteen loads of vegetation is Your stick, and all the world is Your plate. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੇਹੀ ਮਹਜਿਦਿ ਮਨੁ ਮਉਲਾਨਾ ਸਹਜ ਨਿਵਾਜ ਗੁਜਾਰੈ ॥
lines.translit: dehee mahajid man maulaanaa sehaj nivaaj gujaarai |
lines.english: The human body is the mosque, and the mind is the priest, who peacefully leads the prayer.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬੀਬੀ ਕਉਲਾ ਸਉ ਕਾਇਨੁ ਤੇਰਾ ਨਿਰੰਕਾਰ ਆਕਾਰੈ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: beebee kaulaa sau kaaein teraa nirankaar aakaarai |3|
lines.english: You are married to Maya, O Formless Lord, and so You have taken form. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਗਤਿ ਕਰਤ ਮੇਰੇ ਤਾਲ ਛਿਨਾਏ ਕਿਹ ਪਹਿ ਕਰਉ ਪੁਕਾਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: bhagat karat mere taal chhinaae kih peh krau pukaaraa |
lines.english: Performing devotional worship services to You, my cymbals were taken away; unto whom should I complain?

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਮੇ ਕਾ ਸੁਆਮੀ ਅੰਤਰਜਾਮੀ ਫਿਰੇ ਸਗਲ ਬੇਦੇਸਵਾ ॥੪॥੧॥
lines.translit: naame kaa suaamee antarajaamee fire sagal bedesavaa |4|1|
lines.english: Naam Dayv's Lord and Master, the Inner-knower, the Searcher of hearts, wanders everywhere; He has no specific home. ||4||1||
